channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 Party City stores will stay open despite national closures — here's where
item.description: Party City stores in Hawaii will remain open, even as the national chain announced plans to close 850 locations across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Lopez, KTLA 5

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, KREX 5

item.title: Trump taps 'Apprentice' producer, Mark Burnett, as special envoy to the United Kingdom
item.description: Mark Burnett, the power producer who helped reintroduce Donald Trump to a national television audience with “The Apprentice,” is being tapped by the president-elect as special envoy to the United Kingdom in his upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA fines Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla $35,000 for postgame comments toward officials
item.description: The NBA fined the Boston Celtics coach $35,000 on Saturday “for aggressively pursuing and directing inappropriate language toward a game official” at the end of his team's loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL star Michael Vick officially hired by Norfolk State on Friday
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Norfolk State officially named Michael Vick its head football coach Friday, the latest hire of a talented NFL player with no college coaching experience to lead an HBCU program.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Orphaned elk raised by Oregon cows accepted by local elk herd
item.description: An orphaned elk calf that turned up at a Pacific City ranch in March was recently accepted by a herd of elk after spending nearly nine months living with a herd of Angus beef cows.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: The top uniformed police officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 of the most frustrating health insurer tactics and why they exist
item.description: Even for those with health insurance, coverage does not ensure access to care, much less high-quality and affordable care.
item.dc:creator: Monica S. Aswani Assistant Professor of Health Services Administration, University of Alabama at Birmingham, Fox 5

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US civil rights probe faults Philadelphia school district over handling of antisemitism reports
item.description: The Philadelphia school district failed to show it protected Jewish students from harassment despite “repeated, extensive notice” that students, teachers and administrators were engaging in antisemitic behavior, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Government shutdown is averted just after deadline as Congress rejects Trump's debt limit demands
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Farnoush Amiri And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ohio lawmakers approve charging up to $750 for police and jail videos
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — People seeking copies of police and jail videos in Ohio may have to pay up to $75 for each hour if Gov. Mike DeWine signs a measure approved by the state legislature this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks workers plan strikes that could hit hundreds of stores by Christmas Eve
item.description: Workers at Starbucks stores plan to go on a five-day strike starting Friday to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Young nuns: Two diverse paths to a rare life of faith
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Less than 1% of Catholic nuns in the United States today are 30 or younger. Seyram Adzokpa and Zoey Stapleton are two of the young women who have made the rare decision to join a religious community and begin the long process to become nuns. Here are their stories.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, KRON4

item.title: How drone activity could affect US holiday travel plans
item.description: Recent drone sightings around the country have raised concerns over whether drone activity could affect holiday travel
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Immigration drives US population growth to highest rate in 23 years as residents pass 340 million
item.description: Immigration in 2024 drove U.S. population growth to its fastest rate in 23 years as the nation surpassed 340 million residents, the U.S. Census Bureau said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers strike at facilities around the country as Teamsters seek contract
item.description: Amazon says it doesn’t expect an impact on its operations during what the union calls the largest strike against the company in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, ABC 4

item.title: Advocates train immigrants to 'prepare to stay' in the US under Trump
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Winston Leiva rattles off a long list of things immigrants should do to protect themselves against President-elect Donald Trump's promise to conduct mass deportations when he returns to the White House. Make a plan for someone to care for your children if you are arrested.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court removes prosecutor Fani Willis from Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others but did not dismiss the indictment, leaving the future of the prosecution uncertain.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What is 'coffee badging?' Workplace trend seen as protest to return-to-office push
item.description: As the post-pandemic return-to-office push gathers steam, again, workers are quietly mounting a pushback of their own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
